channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump administration halts $1 billion in federal funding for Cornell, $790 million for Northwestern
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than $1 billion in federal funding for Cornell University and around $790 million for Northwestern University have been frozen while the government investigates alleged civil rights violations at both schools, the White House says.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: At least 17 international students, graduates of U of U had their visas revoked
item.description: On Tuesday, a spokesperson for the University of Utah confirmed that about 17 international students or graduates have had their visas revoked as of April 8.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Outrage builds as video shows Idaho police shooting a knife-wielding autistic teenager
item.description: Idaho police officers opened fire from behind a chain-link fence just seconds after exiting their patrol cars and critically wounded a teenage boy — described by his family as nonverbal, autistic and intellectually disabled — as he stepped toward them with a knife, video from a witness shows.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, CLAUDIA LAUER and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Surgery nightmare: Hospital admits mistake after failed cancer surgery attempt
item.description: An investigation is underway at MountainView Hospital in Las Vegas after a cancer patient woke up to startling news in the operating room.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Murphy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge rules in favor of AP in 'Gulf of America' spat
item.description: A federal judge has ordered the Trump administration to restore the Associated Press's full media access, which was suspended after the news organization said it would not follow Trump's executive order to refer to the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Keystone oil pipeline shutdown could quickly lead to higher gasoline prices
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — The nearly 2,700-mile Keystone oil pipeline was shut down Tuesday morning after it ruptured in North Dakota, halting the flow of millions of gallons of crude oil from Canada to refineries in the U.S. and potentially leading to higher gasoline prices.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Spring skiing still thriving at Snowbird as Utah ski season winds down
item.description: Spring skiing remains in full swing at Snowbird, where skiers continue to take advantage of excellent conditions as the season enters its final stretch.
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sonya Massey murder: Judge rules trial can proceed in Peoria County
item.description: Sean Grayson was charged with first-degree murder and fired from the sheriff’s office. He pleaded not guilty.
item.dc:creator: Danny Connolly, NewsNation

item.title: Agreement in works to settle Northwestern hazing lawsuits that led to football coach's firing
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former Northwestern University football players are finalizing an agreement with the school to settle lawsuits alleging hazing and abuse that led to longtime coach Pat Fitzgerald’s firing, a school spokesman and attorneys said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Italy fines Roman Colosseum ticketing agencies $22M for ripping off tourists
item.description: Italy is levying hefty fines against Roman Colosseum ticketing agencies for forcing tourists to pay more to visit the famed historic site.
item.dc:creator: Tammy Chan, KTLA 5

item.title: The trial of an ex-deputy who shot an unarmed Black woman in her home is moved to another city
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — The murder trial of a former Illinois sheriff's deputy who fatally shot Sonya Massey, an unarmed black woman, in her Springfield home last summer will be moved out of the capital city to Peoria because of intense publicity, a judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Feds launch task force to investigate fraud and corruption in Southern California homeless funds
item.description: U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli announced a new task force to investigate alleged fraud and waste involving billions of dollars allocated to address homelessness in Southern California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Dramatic footage shows heat-seeking tech leading rescuers to missing Michigan toddler curled up in ditch
item.description: Watch Michigan State Police rescue a 2-year-old boy who went missing on Sunday. They found him curled up in a ditch on the side of the freeway using heat-detecting technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration says New York must end Manhattan driving tolls this month
item.description: The Trump administration said Tuesday that it's not backing off its latest deadline for New York to end its $9 congestion toll on drivers entering the most traffic-snarled parts of Manhattan, despite a recent court filing that indicated the charge would remain at least through the summer. U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Waiver allows Trump administration to bypass environmental rules to build more border walls
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — A waiver issued Tuesday by the U.S. Department of Homeland Security allows the federal government to bypass environmental regulations and begin construction to add more barriers along the U.S-Mexico border in Southern California even as illegal crossings have plummeted.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Intermountain Primary Children's Hospital opens new Riverton autism clinic
item.description: A new autism clinic opened doors at the intermountain primary children’s hospital in Riverton today, aiming to address a gap in services that people desperately need.
item.dc:creator: Yeonseung Kim, ABC 4

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit is on trial in the US for torture
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A man accused of torturing people suspected in a planned coup against Gambia’s longtime leader was a low-ranking private in the West African country’s military who risked torture and death himself if he disobeyed superiors, a lawyer for the defendant told jurors Tuesday in opening stat
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rapper 'Fat Flow' gets 33 years for kidnapping migrants
item.description: A judge has sentenced a rapper calling himself Fat Flow to 33 years and four months in prison for the 2023 kidnapping of seven migrants in Juarez, Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, KTLA 5

item.title: ICE agent impersonations spike in wake of immigration crackdown
item.description: Impersonatorsposing as immigration agents are spreading fear among migrants already uncertain about their future in the country.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WGN 9

item.title: Spanberger raises $6.7 million in Virginia governor race
item.description: Former Rep. Abigail Spanberger’s (D-Va.) gubernatorial campaign said Monday it raised $6.7 million in the first quarter of the year. The haul included more than 50,000 donations of $100 or less in the first three months of the year, according to the Spanberger campaign.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: The addition of low levels of fluoride to drinking water was long considered one of the greatest public health achievements of the last century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: After days of deluges overfilled rivers to near-record levels across Kentucky, residents are anxious to return to their flooded homes and assess what is salvageable.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner And Kristin M. Hall, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Clem Burke, multifaceted drummer of iconic rock group Blondie, has died
item.description: Clem Burke, whose versatile drumming propelled the iconic rock group Blondie during its decades performing everything from new-wave punk to disco-infused tunes, has died. He was 70. The band said in a statement on its website Monday that he died from cancer but no additional details were provided.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Epic Universe opens a portal to the future of theme parks
item.description: As Universal Orlando Resort gears up for the highly anticipated May 22 opening of Epic Universe, it’s clear the resort is entering a new era - one that could redefine what a theme park can offer families.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KTLA 5

item.title: Catholic bishops end refugee aid partnerships with US government, citing funding cuts
item.description: The U.S. Conference of Catholic Bishops announced Monday that it is ending a half-century of partnerships with the federal government to serve refugees and migrant children, saying the “heartbreaking” decision follows the Trump administration’s abrupt halt to funding.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fan-favorite Krispy Kreme item returning for Easter
item.description: Krispy Kreme is celebrating Easter with an all-new doughnut collection, which includes a fan-favorite item. Starting Monday, fans can enjoy the all-new Hoppy Easter Collection, featuring three festive doughnuts and the strawberry doughnut.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Buying Bitcoin? Here's what to know, according to the Utah Dept. of Commerce
item.description: If you're considering investing in cryptocurrency, the Utah Dept. of Commerce has some advice to help you avoid becoming victim of a scam.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over husband's death
item.description: Lori Vallow Daybell, the Idaho mother with doomsday religious beliefs who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Speaker Johnson strikes deal with Rep. Luna on parental proxy voting
item.description: This marks a development that is set to unlock the House floor after the explosive issue — and the GOP rebellions it sparked — brought the chamber to a standstill.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5
